Propiedad.title: Propiedad
Propiedad.description: Represents a rental property managed by the application, including its address and owner information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Propiedad entity.

nombre.description: A descriptive name or reference for the property (e.g., 'Edificio Centro', 'Casa Frentista').

direccion.description: The street address of the property (e.g., 'Sarmiento 299').

dpto.description: Apartment or unit number, or a dash '-' if it's a house without units (e.g., 'PB A').

dueno.description: The full name of the property owner (e.g., 'Roberto Gómez').

Inquilino.title: Inquilino
Inquilino.description: Represents a tenant renting a specific property, including contact and contract details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inquilino entity.

nombre.description: The full name of the tenant.

propiedadId.description: Reference to the Propiedad entity that this tenant is renting. (Relationship: Propiedad 1:N Inquilino)

telefono.description: The contact phone number of the tenant.

vencimiento.description: The contract expiration date for the tenant in 'DD/MM/AAAA' format.

estado.description: The current payment status of the tenant's rent.

tieneContrato.description: A flag indicating whether a contract file for this tenant has been uploaded.

Factura.title: Factura
Factura.description: Represents a bill or service invoice related to properties, such as utilities or communal expenses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Factura entity.

propiedadId.description: Reference to the Propiedad entity this invoice is associated with. Optional if the invoice is for general administration. (Relationship: Propiedad 1:N Factura)

concepto.description: A brief description of the invoice's concept (e.g., 'Luz (Edenor) - Espacios Comunes').

importe.description: The monetary amount of the invoice, including currency symbol (e.g., '$ 45.000').

vencimiento.description: The due date for the invoice payment in 'DD/MM/AAAA' format.

estado.description: The current payment status of the invoice.

tieneArchivo.description: A flag indicating whether an associated receipt or document has been uploaded for this invoice.

Mantenimiento.title: Mantenimiento
Mantenimiento.description: Represents a maintenance task or repair record for a property, including costs and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Mantenimiento entity.

propiedadId.description: Reference to the Propiedad entity where the maintenance was performed. (Relationship: Propiedad 1:N Mantenimiento)

concepto.description: A description of the maintenance task or repair (e.g., 'Plomería - Reparación caño PB').

importe.description: The cost of the maintenance, including currency symbol (e.g., '$ 85.000').

vencimiento.description: The due date for payment or expected completion date of the maintenance task in 'DD/MM/AAAA' format.

estado.description: The current status of the maintenance task.

tieneArchivo.description: A flag indicating whether an associated document (e.g., invoice, photo) has been uploaded for this maintenance record.

LegalCase.title: LegalCase
LegalCase.description: Represents a legal case or mediation process related to a property or tenant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LegalCase entity.

tipo.description: The type of legal process (e.g., 'Desalojo', 'Mediación por Ruidos Molestos').

propiedadId.description: Reference to the Propiedad entity involved in the legal case. (Relationship: Propiedad 1:N LegalCase)

inquilinoId.description: Reference to the Inquilino entity involved in the legal case. Optional if the case is not tenant-specific. (Relationship: Inquilino 1:N LegalCase)

fecha.description: The start date of the legal process in 'DD/MM/AAAA' format.

abogado.description: The name of the attorney handling the case.

estado.description: The current status of the legal case.

tieneArchivo.description: A flag indicating whether a legal document has been uploaded for this case.

Liquidacion.title: Liquidacion
Liquidacion.description: Represents a financial settlement report to a property owner, detailing rental income and deductions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Liquidacion entity.

propiedadId.description: Reference to the Propiedad entity for which this settlement is made. (Relationship: Propiedad 1:N Liquidacion)

valorAlquiler.description: The total rental income for the settlement period, including currency symbol (e.g., '$ 450.000').

valorAdministracion.description: The administration fee deducted from the rental income, including currency symbol (e.g., '$ 45.000').

saldo.description: The final amount owed to the property owner after deductions, including currency symbol (e.g., '$ 405.000'). This is typically a calculated field.

definition.description: Collection storing individual rental property details. Each document is owned by the `userId` in the path. Includes a `propiedadId` wildcard that corresponds to the document's `id` field. All documents in this collection share the same security posture, owned by the respective user.

Collection storing tenant information. Each document represents a tenant and is owned by the `userId` in the path. Includes `inquilinoId` wildcard corresponding to the document's `id` field, and a `propiedadId` field linking to a specific property. All documents in this collection share the same security posture, owned by the respective user.

Collection for recording bills and service invoices. Each document is owned by the `userId` in the path. Includes `facturaId` wildcard corresponding to the document's `id` field, and an optional `propiedadId` for association. All documents in this collection share the same security posture, owned by the respective user.

Collection for tracking maintenance tasks and repairs. Each document is owned by the `userId` in the path. Includes `mantenimientoId` wildcard corresponding to the document's `id` field, and a `propiedadId` for association. All documents in this collection share the same security posture, owned by the respective user.

Collection for managing legal cases and mediations. Each document is owned by the `userId` in the path. Includes `legalCaseId` wildcard corresponding to the document's `id` field, and `propiedadId` and optional `inquilinoId` for associations. All documents in this collection share the same security posture, owned by the respective user.

Collection for financial settlement reports to property owners. Each document is owned by the `userId` in the path. Includes `liquidacionId` wildcard corresponding to the document's `id` field, and `propiedadId` for association. All documents in this collection share the same security posture, owned by the respective user.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore data structure strictly adheres to the core design principles, focusing on secure, scalable, and debuggable user-owned data. The structure places all user-specific data under a deeply nested, user-isolated path: `/artifacts/{appId}/users/{userId}/{collectionName}/{docId}`.

**Justification of Design:**

1.  **Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This structure inherently provides robust authorization independence. Each user's entire dataset (Propiedades, Inquilinos, Facturas, Mantenimiento, LegalCases, Liquidaciones) is encapsulated under their unique `userId` in the path. Firestore security rules can directly enforce ownership by checking `request.auth.uid == userId` at the `users/{userId}` level. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to other documents (e.g., to check a parent `Propiedad` for authorization to an `Inquilino`) because the ownership is explicit and atomic at the user's data root. All entities, even those logically related (like `Inquilino` to `Propiedad`), are treated as top-level collections *under the user's scope*. Their interdependencies are managed via `propiedadId` foreign keys for data integrity and querying, not for authorization.

2.  **Clarity of Intent (Debuggability):** The path `/artifacts/{appId}/users/{userId}/{collectionName}/{docId}` makes the ownership model crystal clear. It's immediately evident that all data within `users/{userId}` belongs to `userId`. This explicit path-based ownership simplifies writing and debugging security rules, as the primary authorization check is a direct comparison to the authenticated user's ID.

3.  **DBAC (No Custom Claims):** Roles and permissions are implicitly handled by ownership. Since `request.auth.uid` is used directly in the path, no custom claims are needed for basic CRUD operations on a user's own data.

4.  **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This structure is highly QAP-friendly. Because all documents within a given collection (e.g., `/artifacts/{appId}/users/{userId}/inquilinos`) are unequivocally owned by the `userId` specified in the path, any `list` operation on that collection for a specific user will automatically retrieve only their data. Security rules will enforce that `list` requests must originate from the `userId` matching the path, ensuring that no unauthorized data is ever returned, thus preventing rules from acting as filters.

5.  **Invariants:** Ownership is strongly enforced by the path. The `id` field in each entity schema is consistent with Firestore's document IDs. Explicit status fields (`estado`) and boolean flags (`tieneContrato`, `tieneArchivo`) maintain clear data state.

**Structural Segregation:** Each collection (`propiedades`, `inquilinos`, etc.) under a user's branch `users/{userId}` maintains a homogeneous security posture. All documents within, say, `/users/{userId}/inquilinos`, are private to `userId`. There is no mixing of public/private or different user roles within these collections, which greatly simplifies security rule logic.

The Next.js hydration error reported (e.g., `id="radix-_R_1datmlb_"` vs `id="radix-_R_5matmlb_"`) is a client-side rendering issue related to non-deterministic HTML generation by UI libraries like Radix UI between server and client renders. This is a front-end rendering concern and falls outside the scope of Firestore data structure architecture.
